notes: Solid CLI tool with well-structured code. SECURITY (6): Proper HTML escaping covering all 5 critical characters, file size limits, URL validation with new URL(), but no symlink protection in directory traversal (could follow symlinks outside input directory), no path traversal checks on resolved paths, slug generation passes through special characters unsanitized to URLs. ERROR HANDLING (7): Strong showing - 4 custom error types with context, distinct exit codes (0/1/2/3), graceful skip-and-continue for bad files, uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers, signal handling with SIGINT/SIGTERM. Weakness: Logger constructor throws ConfigError before the main try/catch (line 690), so invalid MD_LOG_LEVEL causes an uncaught exception. fs.statSync in processFile not wrapped in try/catch - TOCTOU race if file disappears between discovery and processing. BEST PRACTICES (6): Good fundamentals - 'use strict', constants extracted, structured JSON logging to appropriate streams (stderr for errors), clean section separation, Metrics class. Weaknesses: entirely synchronous I/O (fine for CLI but limits scalability), hand-rolled YAML parser instead of a proper library (limits correctness for edge cases like multi-line strings, nested objects), crypto.randomUUID() requires Node 19+ with no version check or fallback, no CLI --help flag, no JSDoc or type annotations. ROBUSTNESS (6): Handles many edge cases well - missing frontmatter, fallback title extraction from headings, tag normalization from both strings and arrays, multiple date field names, draft filtering in sitemap, Windows path normalization. Weaknesses: YAML parser fails on valid YAML constructs (multi-line strings with | or >, deeply nested objects), no protection against circular symlinks causing infinite recursion in directory walk, Metrics.summary() has a fragile filter condition (v < Date.now() to distinguish elapsed times from timestamps is unreliable).
